title: carpinus nigra
intro: Carpinus nigra, also known as the European black hornbeam, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Carpinus nigra has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and flu to digestive problems and skin conditions.
